meals.name: Skillet Italian Sausage and Bell Pepper Pasta
meals.description: A quick and flavorful pasta dish with savory Italian sausage and colorful bell peppers, ready in about 40 minutes.
instructions: Step 1: In a large skillet or Dutch oven, brown the Italian sausage over medium-high heat, breaking it apart with a spoon. Once cooked through, remove the sausage with a slotted spoon and set aside, leaving about 1 tablespoon of drippings in the pan.

Step 2: Add the olive oil to the skillet if needed. Add the chopped yellow onion and bell peppers and cook for 5-7 minutes until softened. Stir in the minced garlic and cook for another minute until fragrant.

Step 3: Pour in the chicken broth, diced tomatoes (undrained), and marinara sauce. Bring the mixture to a simmer. Add the penne pasta and dried oregano, stirring to combine. Cover the skillet, reduce heat to medium-low, and cook for 15-20 minutes, or until pasta is al dente, stirring occasionally to prevent sticking.
Step 4: Return the cooked Italian sausage to the skillet and stir to combine. Season with salt and black pepper to taste. Serve hot, topped with grated Parmesan cheese.

meals.name: Crispy Baked Ranch Chicken Tenders with Roasted Green Beans
meals.description: Kid-friendly crispy chicken tenders seasoned with ranch, baked to perfection alongside tender roasted green beans, ready in about 40 minutes.

ingredients.name: Ranch seasoning mix (Kinder's The Blend works well)

instructions: Step 1: Preheat oven to 400°F (200°C). Line a large baking sheet with parchment paper or a silicone mat. In a shallow dish, whisk the eggs. In another shallow dish, combine the Panko bread crumbs and ranch seasoning mix.

Step 2: Pat the chicken tenders dry. Dip each tender first into the beaten egg, allowing excess to drip off, then dredge in the Panko-ranch mixture, pressing gently to coat thoroughly. Place the coated tenders in a single layer on one side of the prepared baking sheet.
Step 3: In a medium bowl, toss the trimmed green beans with 1 tablespoon of olive oil, salt, and pepper. Arrange the seasoned green beans in a single layer on the other side of the baking sheet.
Step 4: Bake for 20-25 minutes, or until the chicken tenders are golden brown and cooked through (internal temperature reaches 165°F/74°C) and the green beans are tender-crisp. Serve immediately.

meals.name: Slow Cooker Hearty Beef Chili with Cornbread
meals.description: A robust and flavorful beef chili simmered all day in the slow cooker, served with a side of warm cornbread for a comforting weeknight meal.
instructions: Step 1: In a large skillet, brown the ground beef over medium-high heat until no pink remains. Drain any excess grease and transfer the cooked beef to the slow cooker insert.

Step 2: In the same skillet, add a splash of olive oil if needed. Sauté the chopped yellow onion and bell pepper for 5-7 minutes until softened. Add the minced garlic and cook for 1 minute until fragrant. Transfer to the slow cooker.
Step 3: Add the beef broth, diced tomatoes (undrained), kidney beans, pinto beans, chili powder, cumin, dried oregano, salt, and black pepper to the slow cooker. Stir well to combine all ingredients.

Step 4: Cover the slow cooker and cook on LOW for 6-8 hours or on HIGH for 3-4 hours, until the chili is thick and flavors are well-blended. Prepare the Jiffy cornbread mix according to package directions during the last 30 minutes of chili cooking time. Serve hot with shredded cheddar cheese and a dollop of sour cream, alongside warm cornbread.

meals.name: Quick Honey Garlic Pork Stir Fry Bowls
meals.description: Tender pork and crisp vegetables coated in a savory honey garlic sauce, served over fluffy rice for a quick and satisfying bowl-style meal in about 40 minutes.
instructions: Step 1: Cook the rice according to package directions. While rice cooks, slice the pork into thin strips. In a small bowl, whisk together the soy sauce, honey, minced garlic, and grated ginger. In another small bowl, whisk together the corn starch and water to create a slurry.

Step 2: Heat 1 tablespoon of olive oil in a large skillet or wok over medium-high heat. Add the pork strips and cook for 3-5 minutes, stirring frequently, until browned and cooked through. Remove pork from the skillet and set aside.
Step 3: Add the broccoli florets and sliced carrots to the same skillet. Stir-fry for 3-5 minutes until vegetables are tender-crisp. Add the snap peas and stir-fry for 1-2 more minutes.

Step 4: Return the cooked pork to the skillet with the vegetables. Pour in the honey-garlic sauce. Bring to a simmer, then stir in the corn starch slurry. Cook, stirring constantly, for 1-2 minutes until the sauce thickens. Serve immediately over the cooked rice, garnished with sesame seeds if desired.

meals.name: Speedy Black Bean and Corn Quesadillas with Avocado
meals.description: Cheesy and flavorful quesadillas filled with black beans and corn, quick to make and served with fresh avocado, ready in under 30 minutes.
instructions: Step 1: In a medium bowl, combine the drained and rinsed black beans, drained corn, and taco seasoning mix. In a small skillet, heat 1 tablespoon olive oil over medium heat. Sauté the chopped onion and bell pepper for 3-5 minutes until softened. Add the sautéed vegetables to the bean and corn mixture and stir well.

Step 2: Heat a large non-stick skillet over medium heat. Place one flour tortilla in the hot skillet. Sprinkle half of one side with about 1/4 cup of shredded cheddar cheese, then spoon about 1/4 cup of the black bean and corn filling over the cheese.

Step 3: Sprinkle a little more cheese over the filling. Fold the other half of the tortilla over the filling to create a half-moon shape. Cook for 2-3 minutes per side, until the tortilla is golden brown and the cheese is melted and bubbly. Remove and repeat with remaining tortillas and filling.

Step 4: While quesadillas cook, prepare the avocado by mashing it with a fork and seasoning with a squeeze of lime juice, salt, and pepper. Cut the cooked quesadillas into wedges and serve immediately with the mashed avocado.

meals.name: Sheet Pan Steak Fajitas with Spanish Rice
meals.description: Flavorful steak and colorful bell peppers roasted on a single sheet pan, served with warm tortillas and homemade Spanish rice for an easy weekend dinner in about 55 minutes.

ingredients.name: Flour tortillas (from taco kit or store-bought)

instructions: Step 1: Preheat oven to 400°F (200°C). Line a large baking sheet with parchment paper. Slice the sirloin steak against the grain into thin strips. Slice the bell peppers and yellow onion into thin strips.

Step 2: In a large bowl, combine the steak strips, bell pepper strips, and onion strips. Drizzle with 2 tablespoons olive oil and sprinkle with taco seasoning mix. Toss well to ensure everything is evenly coated. Spread the mixture in a single layer on the prepared baking sheet.

Step 3: Bake for 20-25 minutes, or until the steak is cooked to your desired doneness and the vegetables are tender-crisp. While the fajitas bake, prepare the Spanish rice: In a medium saucepan, combine 1 cup dry rice, 2 cups chicken broth, and 1 (8 ounce) can diced tomatoes. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat to low, cover, and simmer for 15-20 minutes or until liquid is absorbed and rice is tender.

Step 4: Warm the tortillas according to package directions (microwave, oven, or skillet). Fluff the Spanish rice with a fork and stir in chopped fresh cilantro. Serve the hot steak fajita mixture in warm tortillas with Spanish rice, a squeeze of fresh lime juice, and a dollop of sour cream.

meals.name: Hearty Chicken Noodle Soup from Scratch
meals.description: A comforting and wholesome homemade chicken noodle soup, packed with tender chicken, vegetables, and thin spaghetti, perfect for a cozy Sunday meal in about 80 minutes.
instructions: Step 1: In a large Dutch oven or soup pot, heat olive oil over medium heat. Add the chopped yellow onion, sliced carrots, and sliced celery. Sauté for 8-10 minutes, until vegetables begin to soften. Stir in the minced garlic and cook for 1 minute until fragrant.

Step 2: Pour in the chicken broth. Add the chicken breasts, dried oregano, and bay leaf. Bring the soup to a boil, then reduce the heat to low, cover, and simmer for 20-25 minutes, or until the chicken is cooked through and easily shredded.

Step 3: Carefully remove the cooked chicken breasts from the pot and set aside to cool slightly. Discard the bay leaf. Increase the heat to medium and add the broken thin spaghetti pieces to the simmering broth. Cook for 7-10 minutes, or according to package directions, until the pasta is al dente.

Step 4: While the pasta cooks, shred the cooled chicken using two forks. Return the shredded chicken to the pot. Stir in the fresh chopped parsley. Season the soup generously with salt and black pepper to taste. Serve hot.
